title: Steve Kerr explains how the Celtics went from an ‘afterthought’ to a ‘real favorite’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Steve Kerr praises the Celtics' transformation into a contender despite initial setbacks and Jayson Tatum's injury.
The Celtics, currently second in the Eastern Conference, have improved notably in offensive efficiency and team dynamics.
Kerr highlights the Celtics' effective young player development and strategic execution, even without Tatum initially.

title: Advocates sue Worcester over response to mental health 911 calls, alleging discrimination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Advocates filed a federal lawsuit against Worcester, alleging discrimination in response to mental health 911 calls by sending armed officers.
The lawsuit claims Worcester's 911 program sends police to all mental health calls, escalating crises and lacking proper response.
Less than 25% of Worcester police completed crisis intervention training, with no dedicated mental health team since 2023.

title: Hilary Duff will be Northeastern University’s 2026 commencement speaker
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Hilary Duff will speak at Northeastern University's 2026 commencement ceremony in Fenway Park on April 29.
The announcement of Duff as speaker was made through a Y2K-themed social media post featuring the school mascot.
Hilary Duff, 38, is best known for her roles in 'Lizzie McGuire' and her song 'What Dreams Are Made Of.'

title: 3 roster needs Patriots must still address after free-agency spending spree
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots seek a proven No. 1 wide receiver, considering a trade for Eagles star A.J. Brown.
Team aims to improve pass rush after only 35 sacks in 2025, exploring draft options.
Offensive tackle depth is a concern with aging starters and recent losses like Vederian Lowe.

title: Needham 16-year-old charged with murder in Roxbury slaying of woman, 61
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A 16-year-old from Needham charged with murder in the October shooting of Marie Molea-Anthony, 61, in Roxbury.
The teen faces charges of murder, aggravated assault, and unlawful possession of a firearm, police stated.
Boston police continue to investigate the shooting and urge anyone with information to contact them.

title: Chelsea man released decades after murder conviction won’t be retried, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Thomas Rosa Jr. was released after 34 years in prison due to new DNA evidence undermining his 1993 murder conviction.
Suffolk County DA dropped charges against Rosa, citing new forensic evidence, lost evidence, and changes in case law.
Rosa is the 100th exoneree from Massachusetts since 1989, joining the Exoneree Network to support others wrongfully convicted.

title: Former Attleboro man gets 35-40 years in prison for raping 5-year-old niece
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: William Machado sentenced to 35-40 years for raping his 5-year-old niece in Massachusetts.
Machado moved to Massachusetts in 2021 and was convicted on multiple child rape and assault charges.
After the 2022 crime, Machado relocated to Chicago before police arrested him with US Marshals' assistance.

title: A month after tragic shooting, Colin Dorgan, Blackstone Valley hockey triumph to win R.I. state title
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Blackstone Valley hockey team won the Rhode Island Division II state title by defeating Lincoln High School 3-2 in quadruple overtime.
Colin Dorgan scored the equalizing goal in the championship game and helped secure Blackstone Valley's first state title amid personal tragedy.
The team wore jerseys with a heart emblem honoring Dorgan's deceased family members, killed in a shooting last month.

title: Bruins call up former 1st-round pick Lukas Reichel on emergency basis
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins recalled Lukas Reichel from Providence on an emergency basis ahead of a game against the Winnipeg Jets.
Reichel was acquired from the Vancouver Canucks for a 2026 sixth-round pick before the NHL trade deadline.
In three games with the Providence Bruins, Reichel recorded one goal and four assists, totaling five points.

title: World Cup games at Gillette no longer in jeopardy after Foxborough officially approves license
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough officials approved an event license to host seven World Cup games at Gillette Stadium in 2026.
Foxborough Select Board reached a $7.8 million security funding deal with Boston Soccer 2026 to secure the games.
Gillette Stadium will implement a traffic management plan and enhanced MBTA service for 21,000 patrons per game.
